steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two sentences
steps.content:
Sentence 1: Patel's model revealed that supernovas created the Local Bubble and likely triggered star formation. Sentence 2: The model detailed exactly how — the bubble's expansion trapped interstellar clouds that collapsed into stars. Sentence 2 zooms in on the specific mechanism behind the broad claim in Sentence 1. The blank must signal a move from general finding to particular detail.
  • Choice A — Hence,: Signals a consequence or result — but Sentence 2 is not a consequence of Sentence 1; it is a detail within it. ✗
  • Choice B — However,: Signals a contrast — but Sentence 2 supports and elaborates Sentence 1, not contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice C — Admittedly,: Signals a concession — but Sentence 2 adds supporting detail, not a qualification or admission. ✗
  • Choice D — Specifically,: Signals a shift from a general claim to a particular detail — exactly the relationship here. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the transition choice
steps.content: "Specifically" is the only word that correctly marks the move from a broad finding ("likely responsible for the formation of new stars") to the precise mechanism ("how the bubble's expansion trapped interstellar clouds"). The other three transitions all signal relationships — result, contrast, or concession — that are not present between these two sentences. That is the correct answer.
